title: podman system test failure on rawhide: `not ok 21 [410] podman selinux: check unsupported relabel`
body:
This is happening on rawhide only so far. Noticed it in [container-selinux upstream TMT job](https://github.com/containers/container-selinux/pull/345/checks?check_run_id=35989642118https://github.com/containers/container-selinux/pull/345/checks?check_run_id=35989642118) and [podman rawhide bodhi](https://bodhi.fedoraproject.org/updates/FEDORA-2025-0edf5f285c)

EDIT: Moving from container-selinux to podman.

comments.body:
Comment from Ondrej Mosnacek in the SELinux kernel team:
```
It's actually a regression in the "ls" command
(provided by the coreutils package). Some inodes on some filesystems
(e.g. the root inode on tmpfs or some sysfs inodes) don't return
"security.selinux" in the result of the llistxattr(2) syscall, but do
return the value when requested explicitly via lgetxattr(2). This has
been the case since probably forever and it likely wouldn't be easy to
fix it everywhere. The old ls tried lgetxattr(2) regardless of what
llistxattr(2) returned, but the new one trusts the llistxattr(2)
output and assumes there is no label if there is no security.selinux
there.

I'll report it to the upstream SELinux ML and we'll try to come up
with a solution. For now, a workaround is to query the label directly
via getfattr (getfattr -n security.selinux <...>).
```

I have a PR ready. Moving this issue to podman repo.
